rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues have not been addressed by the administrator since his petition for final distribution was initially set for 03/04/2024: 1) The schedule of proposed distribution (Exhibit C) must list a specific amount of reduction and assignment to each assignee. 2) The original payee must be disclosed as to each reimbursement reported on the schedule of disbursements. 3) The petition must be amended and renoticed to request the Court's approval of the petitioner's creditor claim for reimbursement of funeral expenses exceeding $5,000 as additional relief sought. See Probate Code sections 9000(a)(3) & 9252; see also LRSF 14.29. 4) The attorney's filing fees and copying fees paid to the Court and sought to be reimbursed from the estate must be itemized. 5) Please provide a copy of notice of proposed action given before donating the estate's tangible personal property. See section 10513. The information required by CRC 7.250(a) must also be provided. 6) The estate cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. Please file a proof that the cash is now fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). 7) Is the estate ready to be closed, even though the administrator has not recovered proceeds of Macy shares? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KEVIN KA-CHEUNG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Issues were posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 4/8/24. There was no response before the 5/3/24 deadline, so the hearing was continued to 6/17/24. The issues still have not been addressed, although Counsel claimed that she would have something filed within the next two weeks from that 6/17/24 hearing. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The original Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to a post-deceased heir's share must be filed conventionally, with a certified copy of the death certificate attached. [Probate Code Section 11802(c) and LRSF 14.35 G.] (2) The attached schedule from the conservator's supplemental account shows assets on hand as of 10/31/21. The decedent died on 12/27/20. No Corrected Final I&A has been filed to show the assets of this estate. It is unclear how the estate was "appraised" at $602,013.18 in cash. (3) The administrator never petitioned to waive appraisal by the probate referee. [Probate Code Section 8903.] (4) If securities in the JP Morgan IRA accounts had designated beneficiaries and were not assets of the estate, they cannot be included in the fee base. (5) Furthermore, the tangible personal property shown on the conservator's accounting schedule ending 10/31/21 and not separately set forth in the administrator's I&A or in this second amended petition appear to total $13,120.00, not $12,606.00. (6) What books of Japanese illustrations..? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/27/24. If the probate referee needs more time to appraise the assets, Counsel should request a further continuance. Without a proper I&A or an order waiving appraisal by a probate referee, which this Court rarely allows, the statutory fee cannot be correctly calculated. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator was granted IAEA authority on 12/6/21 in the order appointing her; she did not claim her Letters until 1/3/22. The character of property (paragraph 12) is either separate or community, not just "Decedent's property.")

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY TINYEAH FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A Schwab account is not ordinarily just cash. Furthermore, the executor has apparently not actually marshaled the asset, as he proposes to distribute the decedent's account to himself as the trustee, not cash in the estate. The Schwab statement immediately preceding the date of death might need to be filed to support the executor's allegation that all of the assets were cash. (2) An approximate amount is not sufficient. [CRC 7.651 and LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Proposed distribution must show specific assets, including the net amount of cash, to be distributed. (3) If all assets were held in "interest bearing accounts", why is there still the exact same amount on hand as on the date of death? (4) If all of the assets were just cash, as shown in the I&A, the Court will order distribution of $385,642.52 in cash to the trustee. (5) If the assets in the account were not all cash, a Corrected I&A must be filed with the probate referee's appraisal of any non-cash holdings in that Schwab account. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (These issues were emailed to the attorney on 7/9/24, but no response was received by 7/11/24.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IRENE CHRISTINE SANDVIK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as no ex parte petition has been filed to request an order extending special administration, and nothing has been filed since the last hearing on 04/22/2024 to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/10/2024): 1) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. Notice must also be given to the Norwegian Consulate, if it appears that property will pass to citizens of Norway. See Probate Code section 8113. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) Item 6d of the petition must be checked, if the decedent was survived by issue of first cousins (including first cousins once removed or twice removed). 4) The personal representative of the estate of Klara Hauglis must be identified as such by a verified allegation, and notice must be given. If there is no personal representative, all heirs of Klara if she died intestate (who may include Klara's surviving spouse if she was married), or devisees if Klara died testate, must be identified as such by a verified allegation, and notice must be given to each heir or devisee. 5) Direct notice must be given to Turid Bjelland, absent a signed directive stating that notice should be given in care of Turid's spouse. CRC 7.51(a)(2). 6) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed if Else Mundal's address remains unknown. Whether notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (e.g., telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. See CRC 7.52(c), section 1212, and CCP section 413.30. 7) Each factor under section 8465(d)(1)-(4) must be addressed as it applies to the petitioner as nominee of a foreign heir. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANTHONY YUE LUM CHEUNG AND AGNES YIM WAH CHEUNG REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Trustee'S Petition For Instructions Per Prob.Code Section 17200 Et Seq. Regarding Termination Of Irrevocable Subtrusts
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/28/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (The only correct form of notice for a trust petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service of that notice.) (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) No guardian ad litem has been appointed yet, although a notice of appearance of an attorney for a proposed guardian ad litem was filed. (4) An "Amended Declaration" was filed on 6/17/24. As no courtesy copies were provided, all pleadings had to be reviewed online, so it is unclear upon a cursory review, if this is actually an amendment to the petition prayer, which would require the same notice as the petition. [CRC 7.53(a).] (5) It is also unclear if the children had separate legal counsel. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
